text: Dynamic Programming Pattern: 1D DP (Fibonacci-style). Current state depends on a fixed number of previous states. Template: dp[i] = f(dp[i-1], dp[i-2], ...). Space-optimize by keeping only last k values. Use for: climbing stairs (dp[i]=dp[i-1]+dp[i-2]), house robber (dp[i]=max(dp[i-1], dp[i-2]+nums[i])). Time: O(n), Space: O(1) optimized.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: 0/1 Knapsack. Choose items with weights/values to maximize value within capacity. dp[i][w] = max value using first i items with capacity w. Transition: dp[i][w] = max(dp[i-1][w], dp[i-1][w-weight[i]] + value[i]). Space optimize to 1D: iterate w from high to low. Time: O(n*W), Space: O(W).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Unbounded knapsack. Each item can be used unlimited times. dp[w] = max value for capacity w. Transition: dp[w] = max(dp[w], dp[w-weight[i]] + value[i]) for each item. Iterate w from low to high (unlike 0/1 knapsack). Use for: coin change (minimize coins), rod cutting, integer break. Time: O(n*W).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS). dp[i][j] = LCS of s1[:i] and s2[:j]. If s1[i-1]==s2[j-1]: dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1. Else: dp[i][j]=max(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]). Time: O(m*n), Space: O(m*n) or O(min(m,n)) with rolling array. Use for: edit distance base, diff tools, sequence alignment.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Longest Increasing Subsequence (LIS). O(n²) DP: dp[i] = LIS ending at index i. dp[i] = 1 + max(dp[j] for j<i if nums[j]<nums[i]). For O(n log n): maintain 'tails' array, binary search for insertion point of each element. tails[i] = smallest tail element of IS of length i+1. Time: O(n log n).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Edit distance (Levenshtein). dp[i][j] = min edits to transform s1[:i] to s2[:j]. If s1[i-1]==s2[j-1]: dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]. Else: dp[i][j]=1+min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1], dp[i-1][j-1]) for delete/insert/replace. Time: O(m*n). Applications: spell checking, DNA alignment, fuzzy matching.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Coin change (minimum coins). dp[amount] = minimum coins for given amount. Initialize dp[0]=0, rest=infinity. For each coin, for each amount from coin to target: dp[a] = min(dp[a], dp[a-coin]+1). Time: O(amount * coins). For count of ways: dp[a] += dp[a-coin] (order matters) or nested loop (combinations). Space: O(amount).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Matrix chain multiplication. Find optimal parenthesization to minimize scalar multiplications. dp[i][j] = min cost to multiply matrices i..j. For each split k: dp[i][j] = min(dp[i][k] + dp[k+1][j] + dims[i]*dims[k+1]*dims[j+1]). Fill by chain length. Time: O(n³), Space: O(n²). Interval DP pattern.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Interval DP. dp[i][j] = optimal value for subproblem on range [i,j]. Fill by increasing interval length. For each interval length l, for each start i: j=i+l-1, try all split points k. Use for: burst balloons, palindrome partitioning, stone merging, optimal BST. Time: O(n³), Space: O(n²).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Tree DP. Compute DP on each subtree. State defined at each node, depends on children. Post-order traversal: compute children first, then parent. Use for: house robber on tree (rob or not rob each node), binary tree cameras, maximum path sum. dp returns tuple/array of states. Time: O(n).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Digit DP. Count integers in range [0, N] satisfying digit-related constraints. State: (position, tight, [other flags]). Tight: whether current prefix matches N's prefix (limits next digit). Memoize on (pos, tight, flags). Template: dfs(pos, tight, ...) with memoization. Use for: count numbers with digit sum S, count integers with no consecutive same digits. Time: O(digits * states).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Bitmask DP. State includes bitmask representing subset of items used. dp[mask][i] = optimal value having visited set 'mask', currently at node i. TSP: dp[mask][v] = min cost to visit all nodes in mask ending at v. Transition: try adding each unvisited node. Time: O(2^n * n²), Space: O(2^n * n). Use for: assignment problems, Hamiltonian path.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Stock trading with states. Model as state machine DP. States: hold, sold (cooldown), rest. Transitions: hold -> hold (rest) or sold; sold -> rest; rest -> hold or rest. dp[i][state] = max profit at day i in given state. Time: O(n), Space: O(1). Generalizes to: k transactions, transaction fee, cooldown variations.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Palindrome DP. Longest palindromic subsequence: dp[i][j] = LPS of s[i..j]. If s[i]==s[j]: dp[i][j]=dp[i+1][j-1]+2. Else: max(dp[i+1][j], dp[i][j-1]). Minimum cuts for palindrome partitioning: dp[i] = min cuts for s[0..i]. Check palindrome with precomputed 2D table. Time: O(n²). Space: O(n²).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Partition DP (subset sum). Can array be partitioned into two equal-sum subsets? dp[s] = True if sum s achievable. Iterate items; for each, iterate sums high to low: dp[s] |= dp[s-num]. Target = total//2. If total is odd, impossible. Time: O(n * sum), Space: O(sum). Extends to: partition into k equal subsets (bitmask DP).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Grid DP (path counting). dp[i][j] = number of paths to cell (i,j). Base: dp[0][0]=1. Transition: dp[i][j] = dp[i-1][j] + dp[i][j-1] (skip obstacles). For minimum path sum: dp[i][j] = min(dp[i-1][j], dp[i][j-1]) + grid[i][j]. Time: O(m*n). Use for: unique paths, minimum path sum, dungeon game (process bottom-right to top-left).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: String DP (word break, regex). Word break: dp[i] = True if s[:i] can be broken into dictionary words. For each end i, try all start j where dp[j] is True and s[j:i] is in dictionary. Time: O(n² * word_check). Regex matching: dp[i][j] = s[:i] matches p[:j]. Handle '*' by looking back. Time: O(m*n).

Dynamic Programming Pattern: DP with deque (sliding window DP). When transition involves max/min over a sliding window. dp[i] = max(dp[j] + cost) for j in [i-k, i-1]. Maintain monotonic deque of useful indices. Front of deque = maximum dp[j] in window. Amortized O(1) per element = O(n) total. Use for: jump game with max K steps, divide array into K non-decreasing parts.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: Memoization vs tabulation. Memoization (top-down): recursive + cache, only compute needed states, natural code. Tabulation (bottom-up): iterative, all states, better cache performance, no stack overflow. Choose memoization for: complex state transitions, sparse state spaces, when base cases are complex. Choose tabulation for: dense state spaces, space optimization via rolling arrays.

Dynamic Programming Pattern: DP optimization techniques. 1) Rolling array: reduce 2D dp to 1D when dp[i] only depends on dp[i-1]. 2) Knuth's optimization: for interval DP, if opt[i][j-1] <= opt[i][j] <= opt[i+1][j], reduce O(n³) to O(n²). 3) Divide-and-conquer optimization: similar monotonicity condition, O(n log n). 4) Convex hull trick: for DP with linear cost functions, O(n) or O(n log n). Always identify structure before applying.
